spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (LanguageDetector, Translator, detect() with runtime script (~4641 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive surface — 8 buttons, 0 inputs, 0 selects, 0 ranges — wired up via event listeners.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (appears, badge, compose, detected, detects, english, hint, language, translate, type).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus link is present but the page has no dedicated `references` section — the self-heal contract is fragile.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 81 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Wrap references in a labelled `references` section
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, the chromestatus link is the self-heal anchor and should sit inside a clearly-labelled `references` section so the recovery script can find it reliably.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("A JavaScript API for detecting the language of text, with confidence levels.  An important supplement to translation is language detection. This can be combined with translation, e.g. taking user input in an unknown language and translating") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://webmachinelearning.github.io/translation-api/#language-detector-api to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~4641 chars of script across 8 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 4 pass / 1 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
